items.title: Work in log space
items.avoid: Multiplying many Gaussian PDF values directly to compare classes.
items.prefer: Compute log P(y=k) + Σ_j log N(x_j; μ_{k,j}, σ²_{k,j}) and take argmax.
items.why: Log form is standard, stable, and equivalent to the product rule up to monotonic transform.
items.example: Use clf.predict_log_proba(X) rather than manually multiplying 50 PDFs.

items.title: Check feature shape per class
items.avoid: Assuming GNB fits skewed, clipped, or multimodal features without inspection.
items.prefer: Plot per-class histograms; apply log/Box-Cox transforms or switch model family when Normality fails.
items.why: GNB quality depends on Gaussian class-conditional likelihoods being a reasonable local model.
items.example: Income per class is right-skewed → log(income) before GaussianNB often improves scores.

items.title: Respect the independence scope
items.avoid: Adding many redundant correlated columns and expecting the naive product to behave.
items.prefer: Drop duplicates, PCA, or use discriminative models when features are strongly coupled within class.
items.why: Independence assumption is the other half of Naive Bayes — see Conditional Independence Assumption.
items.example: Both radius and area in a circle dataset → keep one or expect double counting.

items.title: Use variance smoothing defaults
items.avoid: Hand-setting σ²=0 or removing sklearn var_smoothing on tiny or clean subsets.
items.prefer: Keep library smoothing; inspect var_ after fit for near-zero entries.
items.why: Prevents infinite log-likelihood spikes when a class has identical values on a feature.
items.example: GaussianNB(var_smoothing=1e-9) is sklearn default — do not set to 0.
